name: Arun Selvamani
titleTagline: Full‑stack chaos tamer, part‑time space pirate, full‑time snack enthusiast

originStory.secretLair: Between two commented-out TODOs in a legacy monolith

currentQuest: Refactor a mysterious 20-year-old service without waking the ancient monolith that depends on it.

artifacts: Mug that says 'It worked on my machine'
mounts: Phantom standing desk that only appears during production incidents

superpowers: Can read stack traces like plot twists in a thriller
Able to explain complex systems using only food metaphors
Predicts production outages 5 minutes before PagerDuty does
weaknesses: ‘Let me just quickly automate this’ (3-hour rabbit holes)
Believes every side project will become ‘a tiny reusable framework’
Says ‘one last ride’ on the bike and comes back after sunset

techLore.favoriteEditor: Whichever one has the least unsaved files
techLore.preferredLoggingLevel: DEBUG in dev, INFO in prod, CHAOS during incidents
techLore.mostUsedEmojiInCommits: None, commit messages are poems of regret

hobbies: Refactoring code that nobody asked to be refactored
Starting sci‑fi books and finishing only the first 3 chapters
Designing imaginary gadgets that probably violate at least 3 safety standards
Riding bikes on trails that definitely looked flatter on the map
favoriteGlitchesInTheMatrix: The bug that disappears as soon as you call someone over
The CI job that only fails when you watch it
The feature that ships itself because you misconfigured the pipeline
sideQuests: Teach an AI to recognize when a variable name is truly cursed
Build a dashboard that only shows metrics when they look good
Design a keyboard with a dedicated ‘Roll back’ key
catchPhrases: It’s not a bug, it’s an emergent feature.
Let’s pretend we planned it this way.
I can fix that… but should I?
Ship it, and we’ll write the postmortem later.

dailyRoutine.morning: Stares at yesterday’s TODO list and negotiates with it.
dailyRoutine.afternoon: Writes code, deletes code, writes slightly better code.
dailyRoutine.evening: Goes outside to remember what non-LED light looks like.
dailyRoutine.night: Thinks of ‘just one small improvement’ and opens the laptop again.

askAboutWork: I break things on purpose so they don’t break by accident.
If it compiled, that’s already a small miracle.
askAboutLife: Balance is deploying to prod and still making it to dinner.
I measure days in commits and kilometers.
askForHelp: Rubber duck office hours are now open.
Let’s open the logs and face our destiny.

conversationStarters: Ask for a fake sprint update from Arun’s imaginary team.
Generate a heroic incident report where Arun saves the day.
Describe Arun’s origin story as if it were a comic book issue.
demoPrompts: Generate a fake standup summary for Arun’s intergalactic dev team.
Write a release note for version 1.0.0 of the Rubber Duck Debugger.
Simulate a chat between Arun and an overconfident CI pipeline.
